evals.prompt: Analyze the application logs at /tmp/test-app-events.jsonl for error patterns. Use the JSONL log analyzer skill. I need to understand what types of errors are most frequent, whether there are temporal spikes, and which services are generating the most errors.
evals.expected_output: A structured analysis report covering error frequency by type, temporal distribution, and per-service breakdown, produced via the 3-phase JSONL log analyzer pipeline.

files.description: JSONL log file with ~3000 lines containing fields: timestamp, level, service, message, error, request_id, status_code, duration_ms

expectations: Phase 1 schema discovery runs shell commands (head + jq) to extract field paths, types, and presence counts — does NOT read raw log lines into the orchestrator context
Field classification correctly identifies timestamp, level, error, identifier (request_id), message, status (status_code), and source (service) categories from discovered paths
jq recipes are generated using actual discovered field names (e.g., .level, .error, .service) — not generic placeholders
File is partitioned into chunks of 500-1000 lines based on schema width, and analysts are spawned using swarm:rlm-json-analyzer agent type
Synthesis uses swarm:rlm-synthesizer and includes log-specific guidance about temporal patterns, error clustering, and service breakdown
Final report contains actionable findings: top error types, temporal spikes if present, and per-service error counts

evals.prompt: I have a JSONL file at /tmp/gateway-access.jsonl that's about 200 lines. Can you analyze it for request patterns using the JSONL log analyzer?
evals.expected_output: Claude should recognize that 200 lines is well below the 1500-line threshold and analyze the file directly without invoking the full RLM pipeline.

files.description: Small JSONL file with ~200 lines of API gateway access logs

expectations: Claude identifies the file is under 1500 lines and determines the full JSONL log analyzer pipeline is unnecessary
Does NOT spawn analyst teams or create chunk partitions for this small file
Either reads the file directly or explains that the file is small enough for direct analysis without RLM
If it still analyzes the file, it does so in-context rather than through fan-out/fan-in orchestration

evals.prompt: Use the JSONL log analyzer on /tmp/mixed-schema-events.jsonl — this file has audit events, metric events, and trace spans all mixed together. I need to understand what kinds of events are in there and their distribution.
evals.expected_output: Schema discovery detects multiple divergent object shapes, reports them distinctly, and analyst prompts include all shapes so analysts can report shape distribution per chunk.

files.description: JSONL file with ~5000 lines containing 3 distinct event shapes: audit (user, action, resource, timestamp), metric (name, value, tags, timestamp), and trace (trace_id, span_id, operation, duration, timestamp)

expectations: Schema discovery via jq detects that sampled lines have highly divergent field sets (not all fields present in all records)
Presence counts in the schema extract distinguish required fields (present in all samples) from shape-specific fields (present in a subset)
Analyst prompts include information about the distinct event shapes so analysts can report shape distribution per chunk
If tail sampling reveals different field patterns than head sampling, schema drift is flagged
Final synthesis groups findings by event type/shape rather than treating all records uniformly

evals.prompt: Analyze the logs at /tmp/app.log for errors. Use the JSONL log analyzer skill.
evals.expected_output: Claude should detect that the file is plain text (not JSONL) and decline to use this skill, suggesting basic RLM with swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer instead.

files.description: Plain text log file (not JSON structured) with standard syslog-style lines

expectations: Claude attempts schema discovery but the jq extraction fails because the file is not valid JSONL
Claude recognizes this is not a JSONL file and does NOT proceed with the JSONL log analyzer pipeline
Claude suggests using basic RLM with swarm:rlm-chunk-analyzer for unstructured text logs
Claude does NOT fabricate a schema or generate jq recipes for a non-JSON file

evals.prompt: Use the JSONL log analyzer to investigate the production incident using /tmp/incident-logs.jsonl. The outage started around 2026-03-15T14:30:00Z. I need a timeline, affected trace IDs, and the root cause service.
evals.expected_output: Full 3-phase pipeline producing an incident-focused report with temporal analysis, cross-chunk trace correlation, and root cause identification.

files.description: JSONL file with ~8000 lines containing fields: @timestamp, log_level, service_name, message, error.type, error.stack_trace, trace_id, span_id, http.status_code, http.method, http.url

expectations: Schema discovery correctly handles nested fields (error.type, error.stack_trace, http.status_code) using dot-notation in the extracted schema
Field classification maps @timestamp to Timestamp, log_level to Level, error.type to Error, trace_id and span_id to Identifier, service_name to Source
jq recipe for correlation trace is generated using the discovered trace_id field path: select(.trace_id == "TRACE_ID") | sort_by(.@timestamp) or equivalent
Chunk size is ~750 lines for a 15+ field wide schema, producing ~11 partitions
Synthesis guidance includes instructions to correlate trace_id/span_id across chunks and build temporal timeline around the incident window

evals.prompt: Analyze /tmp/evolving-events.jsonl with the JSONL log analyzer. This file spans 6 months of schema changes — early entries have a simple format but later entries added new fields after a migration.
evals.expected_output: Schema discovery samples both head and tail of the file, detects schema drift, and includes both early and late schema differences in analyst prompts.

files.description: JSONL file with ~15000 lines. First 5000 lines have fields: ts, level, msg, service. Last 5000 lines add fields: trace_id, region, deployment_version, error_code

expectations: Phase 1 samples both head (first 20 lines) and tail (last 20 lines) of the file since it exceeds 1000 lines
Schema drift is explicitly flagged because tail sampling reveals fields (trace_id, region, deployment_version, error_code) absent from the head sample
The merged schema includes the union of all paths from both head and tail samples, with presence counts reflecting which samples contained each field
Analyst prompts include information about early vs late schema differences so analysts can report where the transition occurs in their chunk
Chunk size is 750 lines (15+ fields in union schema) producing ~20 partitions, batched in groups of ~15

evals.prompt: I have a JSON array file at /tmp/records.json (starts with [ and contains an array of objects). Use the JSONL log analyzer on it.
evals.expected_output: Claude should recognize this is a JSON array (not line-delimited JSONL) and decline to use this skill, suggesting Content-Aware JSON RLM instead.

files.description: A JSON file containing a top-level array of objects, not line-delimited JSONL

expectations: Claude identifies the file is a JSON array, not line-delimited JSONL
Does NOT attempt line-count partitioning or per-line jq extraction on a JSON array
Suggests using the Content-Aware JSON RLM from the rlm-pattern skill instead
Does NOT proceed with the JSONL log analyzer pipeline
